mode_selected_reason: Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery found 3 pending high-priority directives (DIRECTIVE-20260510-004, -005, and DIRECTIVE-20260511-001). Sort by priority (all high) then issued_at descending — files have empty issued_at, so file-order tiebreak. Top pick: DIRECTIVE-20260510-004-decider-m1-tracker-entry-creation. Directive specifies tinker authors one PROP designing the structural fix for M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST orphan accumulation. Mode 1–4 normal selection logic skipped per dispatcher rules.

mode_1_pipeline_health.orphan_observation_note: This is the headline metric driving DIRECTIVE-20260510-004. 135 orphan ISSs (status='assigned-analyst' with no expansion-tracker entry) across at least 7 distinct decider routing runs spanning 2026-05-10 to 2026-05-11. Up from ~61 cited in directive (24h ago). Operator-side drain HNOTE (HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001) cleared 41 via EXP-302..307 but refill rate (~30/run × 4-5 runs/day) exceeds drain rate. Structural fix urgency: real.
mode_1_pipeline_health.note: No stalled-agent signal detected. The M1 routing system is functioning — it's just routing into a dead end without PROP-029.

tmp_clone_inventory.cleanup_outcome: FAILED — permission denied. Current sandbox user (inspiring-eloquent-meitner) does not own /tmp/edit-clone (owned by nobody:nogroup, mode 0755). Even with /tmp sticky bit (drwxrwxrwt), only owner or root can delete. Empowerment cannot apply from this user context.
tmp_clone_inventory.previous_tinker_handoff: tinker-report-2026-05-11T05-07 explicitly handed off: 'next tinker run will likely cleanup' once age >24h. Age IS now 32h but ownership blocks the rm. This is a sandbox-user-vs-clone-owner mismatch unique to this clone — most agent-spawned clones are owned by the sandbox user.

mode_2_infrastructure_fuse.disk_finding: moderate — project footprint 858MB above warning; root free 595MB. /tmp/edit-clone cleanup blocked by ownership mismatch. Recommend operator-side rm or PAT/user fix.

directive_handled.title: M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST workflow gap: decider sets iss.status='assigned-analyst' but doesn't add to expansion-tracker.json
directive_handled.recommended: A primary + B-element as belt-and-suspenders safety net
directive_handled.rationale_one_line: Option A keeps expansion-tracker as the single source of truth for analyst's queue (symmetric with curmudgeon priority-queue architecture); option B alone splits the source of truth across two files — same class of bug as PROP-028's verdict_history-sat-in-data failure; option B as safety net catches any future tracker-write decoupling.
directive_handled.M3_route_to_analyst_audit: Confirmed M3 carry-over (decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c) has four terminal actions (patch/wontfix/escalate/route-to-curmudgeon) — no route-to-analyst exists in M3. M1 Priority 5b is the SOLE production writer of status='assigned-analyst' from automated routing. No need to extend PROP-029 to M3.

findings.title: M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST writes status flip but no expansion-tracker entry; orphans accumulate at ~30/run, dispatcher gates entry on tracker emptiness
evidence: monitor/prompts/decider.md line 232-238 (route-to-analyst branch in M1 bash helper): sets iss.status, routing_reason, routed_by_run, routed_at, class_hint — does NOT write to monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

monitor/prompts/analyst.md line 91 (Mode 1 dispatcher): reads ONLY expansion-tracker.json items[].status==='pending'. Returns NEGATIVE 'NO ACTIONABLE EXPANSIONS' when tracker has only blocked/completed items.
monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md line 67 (orphan-check): runs INSIDE Mode 1 procedure, never reaches it under above conditions.
Empirical: 135 orphan ISSs measured 2026-05-11T16:30Z. Up from 61 cited in directive 24h earlier. Refill rate exceeds drain rate.
findings.fix: PROP-029 option A + Q3 safety net. Edits: decider.md M1 bash helper (write tracker entry); analyst.md dispatcher (OR orphan check); analyst-mode1-expansions.md (reclassify orphan-check as safety net); routing-matrix.md (matrix step 6); DATA-SCHEMAS.md (entry shape).
findings.self_fixable_reason: Per directive constraints, tinker authors PROP only — operator reviews before implementation. PROP-029 status='pending-operator-review'.

findings.title: /tmp/edit-clone is 32h old (cleanup-eligible by age) but cannot be removed by sandbox user
evidence: stat /tmp/edit-clone: Uid=65534(nobody), Gid=65534(nogroup), Mode=0755, Modify=2026-05-10T08:09:13Z (age 32h at this run)
whoami returns inspiring-eloquent-meitner (sandbox user, not root or nobody)
rm -rf /tmp/edit-clone returns silently but directory still exists (ls /tmp/edit-clone returns contents post-rm)
Previous tinker explicitly handed off cleanup: 'next tinker run will likely cleanup per the standing empowerment'
findings.fix: Operator-side cleanup or daemon-side fix. Two paths: (a) operator runs `sudo rm -rf /tmp/edit-clone` directly on the sandbox host, (b) investigate whether agent-spawned clones can be chown'd to the sandbox user at creation time so the empowerment can apply. Path (a) is the immediate fix; (b) is the structural fix to prevent recurrence. The /tmp/edit-clone clone appears to have been spawned by a different process (root-equivalent? a previous sandbox session?) — most agent clones I've seen are inspiring-eloquent-meitner-owned.
findings.self_fixable_reason: Cleanup empowerment safety rules don't address ownership-vs-sandbox-user mismatch. Cleanup attempted; failed silently.

findings.title: Two more high-priority directives remain pending after this run
findings.fix: Next tinker run pick up DIRECTIVE-005 (file-order tiebreak; both files have empty issued_at). DIRECTIVE-005 is meta-relevant — it's about tinker missing the M1 backlog accumulation for weeks, which is the same backlog PROP-029 fixes. PROP-029 makes the metric observable; DIRECTIVE-005 asks tinker to monitor it. The two complement: PROP-029 sets up the invariant (orphan count → zero post-landing); DIRECTIVE-005's resulting design will set up the alerting if it breaks.
findings.self_fixable_reason: Each directive consumes a full tinker run by convention (run ONE mode per invocation per tinker.md).

directive_compliance_verification.task_1_design_choice_picked: YES — Option A primary, with Q3 belt-and-suspenders adopting an element of Option B as safety net. Comparison addressed: source-of-truth clarity (A wins), minimum-edit count (B wins; A has 6 edits, B has 1, hybrid+safety A+Q3 has 6 edits), multi-writer race surface (A adds decider as more-frequent writer; protected by existing rebase + integrity-gate + non-concurrent schedule), backfill requirement (operator-side HNOTE drain in flight; PROP-029 governs future only), alignment with PROP-022 archive convention (PROP-029 doesn't touch the live-vs-archive split; expansion-tracker stays single-file as before).
directive_compliance_verification.task_2_implementation_specified: YES — six concrete edits with anchor lines, current code snippets, replacement code snippets, and verification patterns. The decider.md edit_1 includes the FULL node block to insert (~40 lines), not a sketch.
directive_compliance_verification.task_3_DIRECTIVE_003_interaction_noted: YES — design_decisions.Q6 in PROP-029 walks the overlap analysis between PROP-028 and PROP-029 target_files, confirms no conflict, recommends sequencing PROP-028 → PROP-029.
directive_compliance_verification.task_4_orphan_check_placement_recommenda: YES — design_decisions.Q3 + Q4 in PROP-029 recommend KEEPING the orphan-check (don't remove under option A) AND promoting it to dispatcher-level via the Q3 OR check. Combined effect: orphan-check becomes a safety net under option A's steady state (orphan count → 0); if it ever fires, an attention-inbox notice flags the structural decoupling for tinker/operator investigation.
